vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: Changing the permissions of /etc/gshadow to the STIG‑recommended mode is a standard file‑permission hardening step. It does not modify authentication mechanisms, services, or boot configuration, and the impact is limited to restricting access to the group shadow file, which is low risk and unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect chmod could prevent legitimate group password changes; Potential for mis‑typing the mode leading to overly permissive settings
